ReleaseLabel: dabbler-ai-orchestration 0.41.0 local VSIX (Set 092, Sessions 1-2; no Marketplace publish)
Notes: One checklist for the whole set, walked per session. Walks 1-3 are Session 1 (renderer switch), operator-attested 7/11/2026 - do not re-run. Walks 4-6 are Session 2. FIRST PASS RESULT: Walk 6 PASSED; Walk 4 FAILED (spurious workspace-init strip on this editable-install repo); Walk 5 was deferred pending the Walk 4 fix. The Walk 4 bug is now FIXED (workspaceInitialized = hasAnySets || structureBuilt; a repo with session sets is initialized by construction) and pinned by a new Playwright falsifier confirmed failing pre-fix. RE-WALK NEEDED: reinstall the REBUILT 0.41.0 VSIX, then re-run Walk 4 and then Walk 5. Walk order: Walk 4 in this repo (healthy - no strip), Walk 5 in this repo (modules.yaml break-and-repair), Walk 6 done. Automation at this rebuild: 2,922 Python tests (6 skipped), 1,406 extension unit tests, 23 Playwright Electron tests (the new falsifier included). Install only the local VSIX produced from this working tree; do not publish it.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 - contributed view label and stable layout
Review.ItemLabel: The activity-bar view is labeled Work Explorer without creating a second view
Review.HumanAction:
1. Install the local dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.41.0.vsix when prompted, then run Developer: Reload Window.
2. Open this repository.
3. Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar.
4. Inspect the view header above the session-set tree.
Review.Expectation: The view header reads exactly 'WORK EXPLORER'. There is one Dabbler work view, not a new duplicate beside a retained 'Session Sets' view. Existing saved activity-bar placement remains usable because the internal view ID is still dabblerSessionSets.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: package.json pins name='Work Explorer' while id='dabblerSessionSets'; extension compile/typecheck and the full 21-test Playwright suite passed.

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 - manifest-less repository uses the module tree
Review.ItemLabel: The sole Default module is visible, expanded, and quieter than real module headings
Review.HumanAction:
1. With this repository still open, locate the 'Default' module row in Work Explorer.
2. Confirm its child status buckets and session-set rows are visible without clicking it first.
3. Compare the visual weight/color of 'Default' with normal row names and bucket labels.
4. Collapse Default once and expand it again to confirm the row remains operable.
Review.Expectation: A visible module header labeled exactly 'Default' wraps the status buckets. It starts expanded. Its text is visually de-emphasized (muted description color and lighter weight), but remains readable and has the normal disclosure chevron. There is no legacy flat rendering where status buckets sit directly at the tree root.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: session-sets-tree.spec.ts asserts module-pseudo-default exists, is module-default, starts aria-expanded=true, and rows are aria-level=3; moduleTier.test.ts source-pins removal of implicitOnly/renderBucket(bucket, null).

Review.ItemLabel: A duplicate global set name is loud once per refresh cycle, not repeatedly noisy
Review.HumanAction: Use the prepared automated evidence unless you already have a multi-root workspace with two distinct session-set directories sharing one slug. If you do: open that workspace, run 'Dabbler: Refresh Work Explorer', confirm one warning notification appears, dismiss it, then leave the view open for at least 10 seconds without refreshing.
Review.Expectation: The warning names the duplicate session-set slug and says which directory is shown. The retained winner row carries a red '!' marker with a tooltip explaining the collision. No second notification appears from ordinary repaint/watcher activity during the wait; running Refresh Work Explorer deliberately starts a new refresh cycle and may show one new warning.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: modulesManifest.test.ts proves one deterministic flagged winner across two roots; session-sets-tree.spec.ts renders one winner row with the '!' tooltip in a real multi-root Electron workspace; CustomSessionSetsView resets its notification latch only when refresh clears the scan cache.

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 - healthy repository shows no strip
Review.ItemLabel: No System Status strip renders while every diagnostic is healthy
Review.HumanAction:
RE-WALK after the fix below. Where you are: this repository, fully set up (venv, keys, Python), so every diagnostic is healthy.
1. Reinstall the REBUILT local dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.41.0.vsix (Extensions view > '...' menu > Install from VSIX..., pick tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.41.0.vsix), then run Developer: Reload Window. The VSIX was rebuilt again to carry the Walk 4 fix - reinstall even if you already installed one today.
2. Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar to open Work Explorer.
3. Look at the area between the view header and the 'Default' module row.
Review.Expectation: There is no box or banner titled 'System Status' anywhere in the view. The tree starts directly with the 'Default' module row. Nothing about API keys, Python, Copilot, or workspace initialization is shown.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: systemStatusHtml.test.ts 'healthy state renders no strip'; module-tier.spec.ts 'list-mode repo without a scaffolded router package shows no System Status strip' reproduces this exact scenario (a repo with session sets but no site-packages/ai_router, the editable-install shape) and asserts getByTestId('system-status') count 0.
Review.Feedback: First walk (pre-fix) FAILED: strip showed 'Workspace initialization is incomplete: the virtual environment, ai_router package, and engine instruction files are required.' ROOT CAUSE: this repo installs ai_router editable (pip install -e .), so the structureBuilt scaffold proxy found no ai_router dir under site-packages and reported the workspace uninitialized - even though the repo has session sets and is fully working. FIX: buildSystemStatus now treats a workspace that has session sets as initialized by construction (workspaceInitialized = hasAnySets || structureBuilt), so the false proxy can no longer surface a workspace-init fault on a working repo. Pinned by the new Playwright falsifier (confirmed failing pre-fix, passing post-fix). Awaiting operator re-walk.

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 - invalid manifest is loud but never blanks the tree nor touches the file
Review.ItemLabel: Break-and-repair walk: strip appears, last-known-good tree stays, file is never rewritten
Review.HumanAction:
Where you are: still in this repository, straight after Walk 4. This walk creates a temporary file docs/modules.yaml and deletes it at the end; the repository has no such file today.
1. Create a new file at docs/modules.yaml (right-click the docs folder in the File Explorer > New File...) and paste exactly these three lines, then save:
modules:
  - slug: demo
    title: Demo Module
2. Return to Work Explorer and wait up to 10 seconds. Confirm the tree regrouped: a module header titled 'Demo Module' whose status buckets all show a count of (0), and a module titled 'Unassigned' holding the repository's session sets. No System Status strip is shown - a valid manifest is healthy.
3. Now select the entire content of docs/modules.yaml, replace it with exactly this single line, and save:
modules: [
4. Return to Work Explorer and wait up to 10 seconds.
5. Read the System Status box that appears above the tree, then confirm the tree below it still shows 'Demo Module' and 'Unassigned' exactly as in step 2.
6. Open docs/modules.yaml again and confirm its entire content is still the single line 'modules: [' - the extension must not have rewritten or repaired it.
7. Delete the docs/modules.yaml file (right-click > Delete, it is untracked).
8. Return to Work Explorer and wait up to 10 seconds.
Review.Expectation: Step 5: a box titled 'System Status' appears above the tree with one item reading exactly: 'dabbler-ai-orchestration: docs/modules.yaml is invalid (expected a YAML mapping with a modules list). Fix the file by hand; Work Explorer never overwrites it. Showing the last-known-good module tree.' The tree below it is NOT blank and still shows the step-2 grouping. Step 6: the file content is untouched. Step 8: the strip disappears and the tree returns to the single muted 'Default' module from Walk 4.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: module-tier.spec.ts 'invalid manifest pins System Status and retains the last-known-good tree until repair' drives the same edit-invalidate-repair cycle in a real Electron webview and asserts the file bytes stay untouched; visibleModules.test.ts pins chooseRenderableModuleSnapshot retention semantics.
Review.Feedback: Deferred on the first pass ('I don't know that I should run this until walk 4 is fixed') - reasonable, since a spurious strip would confuse the strip-appears/disappears steps. Walk 4 is now fixed; this walk is ready to run after re-walking Walk 4.

Review.Subarea: Walk 6 - cold-start fault renders in the strip, not in the form
Review.ItemLabel: An uninitialized folder shows the workspace-initialization fault above a warning-free Getting Started form
Review.HumanAction:
Where you are: a brand-new empty folder in a separate VS Code window; this repository stays untouched.
1. Create a new empty folder anywhere outside this repository (for example C:\temp\uat-092-coldstart).
2. In VS Code run File > New Window, then File > Open Folder... and open that empty folder. If asked, trust the folder.
3. Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar.
4. Read the area above the 'Getting Started' heading.
5. Scroll through the whole Getting Started form below it, especially step 1 around the tier radios and the 'Build project structure' button.
6. When done, close the window and delete the folder.
Review.Expectation: Step 4: a box titled 'System Status' sits above the Getting Started form with one item reading exactly: 'Workspace initialization is incomplete: the virtual environment, ai_router package, and engine instruction files are required.' (Your machine has Python and provider keys, so no other item appears.) Step 5: the form itself contains NO inline warning boxes - the old Python / provider-key warning paragraphs that used to render inside step 1 are gone; the strip is the only fault surface. The strip is readable in your current theme and clearly separated from the form.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: systemStatusHtml.test.ts 'renders workspace, Python, and direct-provider faults' pins the literal fault strings; gettingStartedHtml relocation suites assert no data-gs-warning elements render; session-sets-tree.spec.ts asserts the form carries no python warning and no python fault on a healthy runner.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 - contributed view label and stable layout
Walk 2 - manifest-less repository uses the module tree
Walk 4 - healthy repository shows no strip
Walk 5 - invalid manifest is loud but never blanks the tree nor touches the file
Walk 6 - cold-start fault renders in the strip, not in the form
Review.Feedback: I don't know if this is intended, but I still see Steps 2 and 3 in the Getting started.  Eventually, those go away.
